title: Go HTTP Upload and Download Gateways · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for go http upload and download gateways and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for streaming Go file-gateway handler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
Use MaxBytesReader or bounded readers and streaming multipart iteration without ParseMultipartForm disk or memory surprises.
Delay response commitment until safe, set representation headers, stream bounded data, and record partial-write terminal states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 6215 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Gateway Abuse Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Gateway Abuse Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 2545 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend slow bodies, range amplification, decompression, content sniffing, hot keys, tenant exhaustion, and response-header injection.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Gateway Abuse Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Gateway Abuse Defense, keep Gateway Abuse Defense connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend slow bodies, range amplification, decompression, content sniffing, hot keys, tenant exhaustion, and response-header injection.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend slow bodies, range amplification, decompression, content sniffing, hot keys, tenant exhaustion, and response-header injection.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Gateway Abuse Defense, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: The S3 service absorbs all resource-exhaustion risk behind a Go gateway.
